title: EG-9
content:
{{Top|fga|leg|notoc=1}} {{Droid |type= |image=[[File:EG9-EpisodeITVD.jpg]] |name=EG-9 |homeworld= |birth= |death= |creator= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |class=[[Labor droid/Legends|Labor droid]]''[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary]]'' |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |pronouns=new |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |plating=Orange and silver |armament= |equipment= |affiliation= }} '''EG-9''' was an ancient [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droid]] who was mainstay of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. It was working on a [[Moisture vaporator/Legends|moisture vaporator]] shortly before the annual [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|podrace]] of the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. As the [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] ''[[Podracing Tales]]'' takes place concurrently with the events of the film, it too must take place in 32 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]. EG-9 was [[Monster droid/Legends|cobbled together]] from at least three different models of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], including a salvaged [[Temirca droid]] head with a broken [[Antenna/Legends|antenna]], a [[Montoro serving drone]] body, repair worker [[Limb/Legends|arms]], and [[wheel]]s [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenged from]] a [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[G9 droid]]. It was given an [[Color/Legends|orange]] paint job with silver highlights for maximum visibility, and had gray [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]]. EG-9 had a broken antenna attached to the left side of its head. ==Behind the scenes== EG-9 was originally created for the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. However, its short,[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']] humorous scene, while scripted{{DB|droid|pk4|PK droid}} and included in early cuts, was left out of the final version of the film. The [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] reference book [[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary|''Star Wars: Episode I The Visual Dictionary'']], written by [[David West Reynolds (real person)|David West Reynolds]]''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]'' and released in [[May]] 1999,{{CollectorCite|6|Books with Bite}} used an image of EG-9 to depict an unidentified droid, which later appeared, unnamed, in ''[[Podracing Tales]]'', an online [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] published on [[StarWars.com]] on [[December 19]], [[2000]]."[[Podracing Tales]]" This droid was only explicitly identified as EG-9 in ''[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary]]'', a revised edition of ''Star Wars: Episode I The Visual Dictionary'' written by [[Jason Fry]] and released on [[January 16]], [[2012]],{{DK|subdomain=us|url=nf/Book/BookDisplay/0,,9780756689957,00.html|text=Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary}} making the character [[canon]] within the Legends continuity. [[File:EG-9_concept_art.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Concept art of EG-9]] EG-9's likeness was again recycled as the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[MED-47]] [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid]] featured in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' ===Deleted scene=== {{Quote|They must be important if the Viceroy sent one of those useless protocol gearheads to greet them.''"
"''A Republic cruiser! That's trouble…don't you think?''"
"''I'm not made to think.|PK-4 and EG-9, in a scene deleted from ''The Phantom Menace''|[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay'']]}} EG-9's deleted scene from ''The Phantom Menace'' was included in ''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay]]'', released in [[May]] 1999,{{CollectorCite|6|Books with Bite}} which also featured a storyboard of the scene from the [[Lucasfilm Archives]]. An animatic of the scene, dubbed "Trash Talking Droids," was later included in the [[2011]] ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' [[Wikipedia:Blu-ray|Blu-ray]] set,''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' The scene features EG-9 stationed aboard the Trade Federation [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Saak'ak/Legends|''Saak'ak'']]. When the [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|Republic cruiser]] [[Radiant VII/Legends|''Radiant VII'']] arrives carrying [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], EG-9 and fellow worker droid [[PK-4/Legends|PK-4]] watch the [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] from a nearby hallway. PK-4 assumes the Jedi are important when [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] sends [[TC-14/Legends|TC-14]], whom the droids consider a "useless [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol]] gearhead," to greet them. When EG-9 notices the Jedi's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] belongs to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Gender/Legends|he]] asks if PK-4 thinks it could mean trouble, to which the other droid responds that he is not made to think. [[File:PK-4_and_EG-9.jpg|thumb|right|250px|PK-4 and EG-9, in a storyboard of a deleted scene from ''The Phantom Menace'']] With regards to ''[[Star Wars]]'' Legends, [[Lucasfilm]] considered the deleted scenes of the [[prequel trilogy]] films to be canon, unless they conflicted with story details from the films themselves.{{Blog|holocron|33|What'd I say again? Select posts by me regarding continuity}} EG-9's deleted scene shows the droid to be part of the Trade Federation, which ''The Phantom Menace'' establishes has no presence on Tatooine,{{Film|I}} while ''The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary'' states that EG-9 is a "mainstay" of that desert planet. Given these conflicting elements, this article treats EG-9's deleted scene as non-canon. ==Appearances== *{{Film|I}} {{Del}} *"[[Podracing Tales]]" {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{YJCCG|set=Menace of Darth Maul|cardname=Republic cruiser, transport|link=menaceofdarthmaul/light/images/70republiccruisertransport.gif}} *''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]'' *[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace Illustrated Screenplay'']] {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']] *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/galactic/2005/08/galactic20050817.html|series=Galactic Gallery|text=EG-9 Concept}} *''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' *{{DB|droid|pk4|PK droid}} *''[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of EG-9}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=EG-9 |fr=EG-9 |nl=EG-9 |pl=EG-9 }} [[Category:General labor droids]] [[Category:Junk droids]]
